findings.title: ActivityState uses boolean flag + empty string sentinels
findings.description: Encapsulation: 5/10, Expression: 4/10, Usefulness: 6/10, Enforcement: 3/10 (Aggregate: 4.5). ActivityState allows illegal combinations: isActive=false with currentApp='VS Code', or isActive=true with currentApp=''. The empty-state branch in getState() returns currentApp:'', lastActivityTimestamp:0 -- sentinel values that callers must know to check for. A discriminated union (ActiveState | InactiveState) would make illegal states unrepresentable. This is the same boolean-flag anti-pattern previously identified in TriggerResult.
findings.suggestion:
Replace with a discriminated union:

type ActivityState = ActiveState | InactiveState

interface ActiveState {
  status: 'active'
  currentApp: string  // non-empty
  currentWindowTitle: string
  continuousWorkDurationMs: number
  recentApps: string[]
  lastActivityTimestamp: number
  isFullscreen: boolean
}

interface InactiveState {
  status: 'inactive'
  recentApps: string[]
  lastActivityTimestamp: number | undefined
}

findings.title: ActivityMonitor accepts invalid configuration without validation
findings.description: Encapsulation: 8/10, Expression: 5/10, Usefulness: 7/10, Enforcement: 4/10 (Aggregate: 6.0). Constructor accepts any number for interval/threshold options including negative, zero, or NaN values. A negative aggregationIntervalMs or zero eventRetentionMs would cause incorrect behavior (setInterval with negative value, all events immediately trimmed). As an Imperative Shell boundary, this should validate inputs.

findings.title: Bare number type for timestamps and durations across new types
findings.code_snippet:
continuousWorkDurationMs: number
recentApps: string[]
lastActivityTimestamp: number
findings.description: Encapsulation: 5/10, Expression: 6/10, Usefulness: 7/10, Enforcement: 3/10 (Aggregate: 5.25). ActivityState.continuousWorkDurationMs, ActivityState.lastActivityTimestamp, ProcessedContext.timestamp, and ActivityMonitorOptions interval fields all use bare `number`. There is no type-level distinction between a timestamp-in-ms and a duration-in-ms -- callers can pass a duration where a timestamp is expected, or mix seconds with milliseconds. Branded types (e.g., `type TimestampMs = number & { __brand: 'TimestampMs' }`) would catch these at compile time.

findings.title: VlmResult.activity uses plain string instead of literal union
findings.description: Encapsulation: 5/10, Expression: 4/10, Usefulness: 6/10, Enforcement: 3/10 (Aggregate: 4.5). VlmResult.activity is documented via JSDoc as having known categories ('coding', 'browsing', 'writing') but typed as plain string. A string literal union would enable exhaustive pattern matching by consumers. However, since VLM output is inherently open-ended (model may return unexpected categories), plain string may be acceptable -- the concern is lower severity. ProcessedScreenshotContext.activity inherits this same looseness.
findings.suggestion:
Consider a known-values-plus-escape-hatch pattern:

type ActivityCategory = 'coding' | 'browsing' | 'writing' | 'gaming' | 'communication' | (string & {})

This preserves autocomplete for known values while allowing unknown VLM outputs.

positive_observations: ActivityMonitor has excellent encapsulation: all fields private, configuration readonly, state mutation only through well-defined methods (recordEvent, setScreenshotContext, tick)
Clean Functional Core / Imperative Shell separation: ActivityMonitor (Shell) delegates to pure buildActivityContext function (Core) for event aggregation
ProcessedContext cleanly composes ActivityState with optional ProcessedScreenshotContext, good use of optional property for screenshot availability
Error handling in tick() properly wraps callback errors with Error cause chain and reports via onError callback rather than swallowing or crashing
Event window management (trimEvents) prevents unbounded memory growth with configurable retention
